title: API Reference
description: Comprehensive reference for all NASS API endpoints, including request and response formats.

content.title: Connect Using NodeJS
content.description: Guide to connect your NodeJS application to the NASS API using the official NASS connection package.

vocabulary.definition: The Application Programming Interface provided by NASS for developers to connect their services and applications.
A unique key provided to each service that allows developers to authenticate requests made to the service while in development mode.
A key that identifies actions performed by the developer, used for logging, verifying rights, and supporting secure project development.
An official package provided by NASS to facilitate the connection between your application and the NASS API.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content:
This section provides an overview of how to connect your NodeJS application to the NASS API using the official NASS connection package.

Pre-requisites

Before you can connect your NodeJS application to the NASS API, ensure you have the following:

  • A registered service in NASS with developer credentials (Private API Key and Private Developer Key).
  • NodeJS installed on your development machine.
  • Basic knowledge of JavaScript and NodeJS.
  • An understanding of your application's architecture and how it will interact with the NASS API.
  • A working environment for testing your API connections.
